general_provisions: This bill addresses oversight of the Office of Inspector General of Medicaid Services.
hilighted_provisions: This bill:<hr><ltbullet>establishes the Office of Inspector General of Medicaid Services (office) as an office within the Department of Government Operations (department);<hr><ltbullet>removes the office as an independent entity subject to Title 63H, Independent State Entities;<hr><ltbullet>amends the office's audit and other responsibilities;<hr><ltbullet>requires the office to submit a budget for the office to the department;<hr><ltbullet>requires the executive director of the department (executive director) to:<hr><ltbullet1>establish performance metrics for the office;<hr><ltbullet1>establish a process for employees and members of the public to report concerns to the executive director;<hr><ltbullet1>report the concerns to an advisory board; and<hr><ltbullet1>report to the advisory board on the office's performance based on performance metrics; <hr><ltbullet> requires the inspector general of Medicaid services (inspector general) to:<hr><ltbullet1>submit an annual report to the Social Services Appropriations Subcommittee; <hr><ltbullet1>collaborate with the Office of the Legislative Auditor General; and<hr><ltbullet1>present certain information at meetings of the Social Services Appropriations Subcommittee and the Health and Human Services Interim Committee;<hr><ltbullet>requires the executive director to create an advisory board to:<hr><ltbullet1>promote coordination of Medicaid program integrity activities; <hr><ltbullet1>make recommendations regarding audit prioritization to the office and the department;<hr><ltbullet1>review employee concerns reported to the executive director; <hr><ltbullet1>make recommendations regarding improving the office's performance to the inspector general, the executive director, and the Legislature;<hr><ltbullet1>review the office's annual risk assessment and the office's annual audit plan;<hr><ltbullet1>review limitations that impede the office's ability to appropriately conduct audits; and<hr><ltbullet1>review agencies' implementation of the office's audit recommendations; <hr><ltbullet>provides a sunset date for the advisory board and related provisions;<hr><ltbullet>defines terms; and<hr><ltbullet>makes technical and conforming changes.
